contraventionDescription: Parked in a designated disabled person’s parking space without clearly displaying a valid disability badge. Contravention Code 40

sections.heading: Parked in a designated disabled person’s parking space without clearly displaying a valid disability badge. Contravention Code 40
sections.content:
Only blue badge holders may park in a disabled parking space
Also sometimes listed as: Parked in a designated disabled person’s parking place without displaying a valid disabled person’s badge in the prescribed manner
sections.contentHtml:
Only blue badge holders may park in a disabled parking space

Also sometimes listed as: Parked in a designated disabled person’s parking place without displaying a valid disabled person’s badge in the prescribed manner

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
1. Disabled bays must have a sign adjoining the bay stating that the bay is for “disabled badge holders only”. Some bays operate “at any time” and others operate at specific times which must be clearly stated on the roadside signage. However the words “At any time” are not an approved phrase for the sign listed below. So unless special permission has been obtained from the department of transport then any bays with the sign below with the words “at any time” added may be unenforceable. For details of the signs and specific bay marking required please see details and diagrams below.
Below we show diagrams of the signs and bay markings which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice. It may be that you were confused where you parked.
Knowingly parking in a disabled bay is not something that we would condone.
2. Usually disabled parking bays are well marked with the words “disabled” painted on the outside of the bay. In fact this is a recommendation by the department for transport If the bay is not so marked and you did not know that the bay was for disabled badge holders only then you should mention this in your representation. Also a disabled bay must be an absolute minimum of 6600mm long and 1800mm wide in order to comply with the regulations. This is larger than a normal parking bay. Transport for London’s disabled bays should be 2,700mm wide.
3. In London some local authorities have their own rules regarding disabled parking. For example in London Westminster Council, Kensington and Chelsea council, the city of London and part of Camden operate a completely different white badge scheme as they do not accept blue badges for parking free in pay and display bays or for parking on single and double yellow lines. However you can park in disabled bays with a blue badge. This leads to confusion amongst motorists and many receive penalty charge notices. If you are in doubt always check the council’s website for clarification.
4. Many disabled bays allow parking for a maximum period at certain times. This is usually 4 hours. Obviously if you were not parked for more than the maximum period then you should appeal. You should ask to see the Civil enforcement officer’s (CEO’S) notes. These should clearly show that you were parked in the bay at various times. Usually you can return to the bay after a certain period which is often one hour. So if there is a gap of more than an hour in the notes did you leave the space and return. To enforce this some CEO’s make a note of the valve positions on a vehicle’s tyres when making notes.
5. Please remember that you may also cite compassionate grounds when making your representation to the local authority.
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
 
Below: Signs that should be in situ at the point of parking or within 3 car lengths of where you parked. Please note the sizes and permitted variants.

 
Below: Bay markings which should be in place on the road. Again check the minimum size of the bay and the other details.
Back to Parking
